column.name: Actual_Address
column.description: Free-text geocoded/entered street address for the client outlet, often including locality, city, state, PIN, and country or sometimes just lat/long; use as the most detailed address but expect inconsistencies and user-entered noise.

column.name: AddCity
column.description: City associated with the client address for reporting and territory mapping (e.g., Guwahati, Thrissur); not always normalized and may differ from distributor city.

column.name: AddState
column.description: State/region tag for the client address, often using internal state codes or zone-specific labels (e.g., MP-BHP, NESA, West Bengal - North); use for regional rollups but validate against a master list if needed.

column.name: AssignEmp
column.description: Optional field indicating an assigned employee/user for the client (often null in samples); treat as a legacy or workflow assignment field rather than the authoritative owner unless confirmed.

column.name: BeatDate
column.description: Date/time when the client was tagged to a beat/route or when beat mapping was last updated (string-formatted, sometimes blank); interpret carefully as it is not a typed datetime and may be missing.

column.name: BeatDistributorId
column.description: Identifier of the distributor associated with the beat mapping (typically numeric as string, often blank when not on a beat); useful to join to distributor master when analyzing route ownership.

column.name: BeatId
column.description: Identifier for the beat/route the client belongs to (string, may be blank); use to group clients by route for coverage and visit planning analyses.

column.name: BeatOwnerEmp_Id
column.description: Employee ID of the beat owner/primary responsible rep for the beat (often null/blank when unmapped); use for beat-level accountability rather than client creator.

column.name: BeatOwnerName
column.description: Name of the beat owner (often 'null' or a person name); treat as a denormalized display field and prefer BeatOwnerEmp_Id/employee master for analytics.

column.name: BeatType
column.description: Cadence/type of beat such as Monthly or abbreviated codes like 'M' (can be blank); use to understand expected visit frequency and route planning assumptions.

column.name: Beats
column.description: Human-readable beat/route name (e.g., 'Bistupur Market -Jamshedpur'); good for dashboards but not reliable as a unique key compared to BeatId.

column.name: ClientDivision
column.description: Additional division classification for the client (e.g., 'Parlour,Both'), sometimes blank; use to segment clients when they span multiple business divisions beyond the primary Division field.

column.name: ClientSSId
column.description: Source-system/client-subsystem identifier for the client or related party (bigint); commonly used to track the same entity across systems and should be treated as an alternate key, not necessarily unique globally without context.

column.name: ClientSSName
column.description: Name corresponding to ClientSSId (often distributor/stockist name like 'Om Marketing'); treat as the source-system name and expect '(Deleted)' markers or stale values.

column.name: ClientSource
column.description: Upstream source label from which the client was created/linked (often includes distributor + channel like 'X-Retail' and may include '(Deleted)'); use to attribute onboarding origin but avoid using as a key due to free-text variations.

column.name: ClientSourceCode
column.description: Optional code for the client source (often blank or 'null'); if populated, it may provide a cleaner source categorization than ClientSource free text.

column.name: ClientSourceId
column.description: Numeric identifier for the source entity (often the distributor/stockist ID) used to trace client origin (bigint); use with ClientSourceType to join to the correct master data set.

column.name: ClientSourceType
column.description: Type of the source entity creating/owning the client record (e.g., 'Distributor'); use to interpret ClientSourceId and select the correct dimension for joins.

column.name: Client_Id
column.description: Primary unique identifier for the client/outlet record (bigint, indexed); always filter/join on this field for performant access to a specific client.

column.name: Client_Name
column.description: Display name of the client/outlet (may include '(Deleted)' indicating deactivated/removed); use for reporting labels but do not assume it implies active status or uniqueness.

column.name: Client_Type
column.description: Business type/category of the client such as Retailer or Parlor; use as the primary outlet channel segmentation for sales/coverage analysis.

column.name: Created_By
column.description: User/employee identifier who created the client record (stored as text, often numeric); interpret as creator/auditor field, not necessarily current owner.

column.name: Creation_Date
column.description: Timestamp when the client record was created (string-formatted like 'Apr 3 2021 11:43AM'); parse to datetime for time-series analysis and handle inconsistent spacing/formatting.

column.name: Designation
column.description: Job title of the employee associated with Emp_Id at the time of record context (e.g., SO, Sr. TSI, TSO, Technician); use for workforce mix analyses but confirm against HR master for current designation.

column.name: Division
column.description: Primary business division for the client such as Retail or Parlor; use for high-level business reporting and to align with channel-specific processes.

column.name: DstrCity
column.description: Distributor city or servicing/parent distributor location city (can be blank); use to analyze distributor footprint but note it may not match the client’s AddCity.

column.name: ECO
column.description: Text flag indicating ECO status (e.g., 'No'); treat as a legacy string version of IsECO and prefer IsECO for boolean logic when consistent.

column.name: EmpState
column.description: State/region assigned to the employee (often same code set as AddState); use for salesforce territory reporting rather than client geography.

column.name: EmpZone
column.description: Zone/region grouping for the employee (e.g., West, East-2, East-1, North, South); useful for organizational rollups and performance reporting.

column.name: Emp_Id
column.description: Employee identifier associated with the client record (bigint, indexed), typically the creator or responsible field rep depending on process; always filter/join on this field for performant rep-level extracts.

column.name: Emp_Name
column.description: Name of the employee corresponding to Emp_Id (may include '(Deleted)'); treat as a denormalized label and join to employee master for current/clean names.

column.name: FirstCheckInDate
column.description: Earliest recorded check-in date for the client (typed date); use to measure onboarding/activation timing and time since first field activity.

column.name: Folder_Name
column.description: Optional folder/grouping name used in the application for organizing clients (often null); treat as UI metadata that may be sparsely populated.

column.name: FullAddress
column.description: Concatenated address string assembled from components (city/state/country and sometimes PIN), often less detailed than Actual_Address; use as a standardized-ish display field but expect missing parts and inconsistent formatting.

column.name: Function
column.description: Optional employee function/department field (often null/blank); use only if validated for population and standard categories.

column.name: HQState
column.description: Headquarter state/territory for the employee/client mapping (e.g., Kerala, Jharkhand, 'Other', or a city like Udaipur); interpret as organizational HQ assignment, not necessarily the client’s physical state.

column.name: IsECO
column.description: Boolean indicator of ECO eligibility/status for the client (False/True); use for filtering ECO program participants, noting it may duplicate ECO string flag.

column.name: IsREC
column.description: Boolean indicator related to REC classification/eligibility (True/False); use to segment clients under the REC program/process, and validate business definition with stakeholders.

column.name: Label
column.description: External/legacy label or code for the client (often numeric or alphanumeric like CAN351687, GS10595205); useful as a human-facing identifier and for cross-referencing in operational systems.

column.name: LastOrderDate
column.description: Date of the most recent order placed by the client (typed date, may be null); use to measure recency, churn risk, and active vs inactive outlets.

column.name: LastVisitBy
column.description: Identifier of the user/employee who last visited the client (often numeric as string); interpret as a pointer to employee master and validate against LastVisitEmp_Id consistency.

column.name: LastVisitDate
column.description: Date of the last recorded visit/check-in (stored as string like '27/08/2021'); parse carefully (likely dd/MM/yyyy) and expect nulls or inconsistent formats.

column.name: LastVisitEmp_Id
column.description: Employee ID of the last visiting rep (stored as varchar even when numeric); use as the authoritative rep reference for last visit analysis, converting to bigint as needed.

column.name: OTPVerifyDate
column.description: Date/time when OTP verification was completed for the client (often null); use to assess KYC/verification completion timing where applicable.

column.name: PositionCode
column.description: Sales position/territory code for the employee/client mapping (e.g., NER_WB-N_FF2); use for hierarchy/territory joins and to align reporting to org structure.

column.name: Reference
column.description: Free-text reference field for onboarding or internal notes (often null); treat as unstructured metadata and not suitable for primary filtering.

column.name: Speciality
column.description: Client specialty/segment detail (often null); if populated, use for finer segmentation (e.g., salon specialty) but expect sparse coverage.

column.name: TLName
column.description: Team leader name for the employee hierarchy (e.g., 'Pintu Baruah', 'vacant-...'); use as a display attribute and prefer a hierarchy table for stable TL mapping.

column.name: Varified
column.description: Verification/approval status text and audit trail (e.g., 'Waiting for approval..' or 'Yes, Approved by ...'); use to filter approved vs pending clients but expect free-text variability and spelling inconsistencies.

column.name: pId
column.description: Internal surrogate/process identifier for the record (bigint); treat as a technical ID that may relate to the source/load process rather than the business client key (Client_Id).
